meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelance Pitching: Solve Debugging Roadblocks (Advanced Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with code debugging. Learn how to leverage freelance pitching and AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional portfolios.
content.answer_block: When you hit a debugging wall, stop coding and pivot to freelance pitching. By pitching your services to solve specific business problems, you force yourself to master the stack under pressure. Use Claude Code to audit your logic and Antigravity to architect scalable solutions, transforming technical hurdles into high-value client deliverables.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a cryptic stack trace, a silent testament to hours spent chasing a phantom bug. You are paralyzed, your momentum stalled by a single logic error that feels like an insurmountable mountain. Every developer knows this friction—the gap between the vision in your head and the reality of a broken build.

But here is the secret: you aren't stuck because you lack skill; you are stuck because you lack context. By shifting your focus from 'debugging code' to 'pitching solutions,' you change the game. You move from a student struggling with errors to a consultant solving real-world business problems. When a client's revenue is on the line, debugging stops being a chore and becomes an engineering mandate.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Identify a niche business problem that your current project solves. Pitching requires you to articulate why the code exists, which helps you spot logic gaps.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project architecture and draft a 3-paragraph executive summary explaining the ROI for a potential client in the logistics sector.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --read src/ --prompt 'Summarize the core business logic and identify potential points of failure for a client audit.'

steps.title: Audit for Scalability
steps.description: Before pitching, your code must be robust. Use AI to stress-test your architecture, ensuring it won't break when a client puts it under load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a deep-dive security and performance audit on my main controller; identify bottlenecks that would concern a CTO.

steps.title: Refactor for Readability
steps.description: Clients buy clarity. Refactor your complex functions into modular, documented components that look like professional-grade engineering.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current codebase to follow clean architecture principles, ensuring every function is self-documenting and test-driven.

steps.title: Construct the Technical Pitch
steps.description: Draft a pitch deck that focuses on the 'How'—demonstrating your technical maturity by explaining how you resolved the exact bugs that once slowed you down.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a technical outreach email that highlights my ability to handle complex debugging and system maintenance, using my current project as a case study.

steps.title: Implement the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Use real-world client feedback to continuously refine your debugging process, turning every bug report into a feature improvement.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a systematic debugging workflow based on the common failure patterns found in my project logs.

pro_tips: Never show a client raw error logs; translate them into 'System Optimization Opportunities'.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your bug-prone modules; clients pay for stability, not just features.
When pitching, focus on the 'Time to Market' your code saves them, not the specific tech stack.
Always maintain a 'Debug Diary'—documenting how you solved a bug is the best content for your developer portfolio.
Leverage Antigravity to abstract away boilerplate, allowing you to focus on the unique business logic that clients actually value.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, deploy your project to a staging environment and reach out to three local businesses with your refined pitch.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation and the professional mindset to stop chasing errors and start building a career. Don't let a missing semicolon define your potential. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
